title: Saturdays Of Thunder

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's reaction to something disappointing or annoying
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfectly economical character expression that became a cultural phenomenon

jokes.text: I'm actor Troy McClure. You might remember me from such TV series as... Buck Henderson, Union Buster... and Troy and Company's Summertime Smile Factory
jokes.setup: Troy McClure's standard introduction formula
jokes.punchline: Absurd, specific fake TV show titles
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy McClure formula with specific absurd titles that sound plausible yet ridiculous

jokes.text: Hi, everybody. Hi, Dr. Nick.
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick's greeting
jokes.punchline: The crowd's enthusiastic response to an incompetent doctor
jokes.explanation: Dr. Nick's signature greeting - perfectly captures his unearned confidence and public adoration

jokes.text: With one application of Spiffy... you'll think the body's still warm
jokes.setup: Cleaning product demonstration on Edgar Allan Poe's tombstone
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely inappropriate sales pitch about making a grave seem fresh
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny subversion of infomercial language applied to death - perfectly inappropriate for Dr. Nick

jokes.text: With one application of Spiffy... you'll think the body's still warm.
jokes.setup: Cleaning product effectiveness claim
jokes.punchline: Morbid implication about making gravestone look fresh
jokes.explanation: Dark humor payoff to the gravestone setup - unexpected morbid sales pitch

jokes.text: Quoth the raven-- What a shine!
jokes.setup: Edgar Allan Poe reference on his cleaned tombstone
jokes.punchline: Mixing literary reference with cleaning product enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Clever literary reference mixed with product pitch - good wordplay execution

jokes.text: If stuff starts flying just turn your head. Check.
jokes.setup: Bart can't find safety goggles for power saw
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual dismissal of basic safety with Bart's acceptance
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer parenting failure - casual endangerment treated as normal advice

jokes.text: Patty, Thelma, what a pleasant surprise!
jokes.setup: Homer being told to be nice to Marge's sisters
jokes.punchline: Gets one sister's name completely wrong while feigning enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt to be nice immediately undermined by not knowing Selma's name - perfect character moment

jokes.text: What do you know. He's wearing pants. I owe you lunch.
jokes.setup: Sisters visiting Homer
jokes.punchline: They had bet on whether Homer would be dressed
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-based insult - implies Homer's standards are so low that basic clothing is betting-worthy

jokes.text: Ugly. / Ugly. Butch.
jokes.setup: Sisters commenting on hairstyles
jokes.punchline: Brutal dismissal escalating to personal insult
jokes.explanation: Mean-spirited character consistency but lacks wit or originality

jokes.text: Expiration date, June 1989... uh, 2012, yeah.
jokes.setup: Homer checking expiration date on product
jokes.punchline: Obvious lie about 23-year-old expired product
jokes.explanation: Homer's transparent lie about expired product - shows his self-deception and poor math skills

jokes.text: Shh! They'll hear you.
jokes.setup: Marge asks if he's ordering junk off TV
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks the TV people can hear them through the screen
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike misunderstanding of how television works - perfectly in character

jokes.text: You can't weld with such a little flame. Stupid kid.
jokes.setup: Homer watching Bart work with tools
jokes.punchline: Homer criticizing welding technique while knowing nothing about it
jokes.explanation: Homer's unearned expertise and casual insult to his son - typical parenting failure

jokes.text: I got me a future. I'm two days away from retirement. My daughter's graduating from college. Little Suzie is growing up.
jokes.setup: Partner listing all his reasons to live and future plans
jokes.punchline: Classic 'doomed cop' movie setup
jokes.explanation: Standard action movie trope parody - predictable but serviceable setup for the death

jokes.text: Mendoza!
jokes.setup: Partner dies asking McBain to get Mendoza
jokes.punchline: McBain's dramatic, over-the-top cry for vengeance
jokes.explanation: Classic action movie revenge cry - McBain's over-the-top delivery parodies the genre

jokes.text: You want to rent it, sir? Why? I just saw the best part.
jokes.setup: Clerk asking if Homer wants to rent the action movie
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact dismissal since he saw the climax
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about spoiling movies - Homer's practical logic is unexpectedly sound

jokes.text: Just grab something. All these movies are great.
jokes.setup: Lisa can't find her movie at video store
jokes.punchline: Homer's blanket endorsement of all movies despite having no standards
jokes.explanation: Homer's lack of discernment in entertainment - perfectly in character for someone with no taste

jokes.text: Maybe you should try it on Homer. He'll fail it.
jokes.setup: Fatherhood test magazine
jokes.punchline: Patty's casual certainty that Homer will fail as a father
jokes.explanation: Patty's casual cruelty and accurate assessment of Homer - sets up the episode's main conflict

jokes.text: I don't always keep my cool like the Fonz... but my love for my kids has given me plenty of happy days.
jokes.setup: Henry Winkler quote about fatherhood
jokes.punchline: References both his Fonzie character and Happy Days show
jokes.explanation: Decent celebrity reference joke that works both character names into parenting advice

jokes.text: Oh, great. You made me miss Joe Theisman.
jokes.setup: Marge interrupting Homer's TV watching for the test
jokes.punchline: Homer prioritizing football injury footage over family interaction
jokes.explanation: Homer's skewed priorities - famous injury more important than parenting assessment

jokes.text: Well, there's the fat kid with the thing. The little wiener who's always got his hands in his pockets.
jokes.setup: Asked to name one of Bart's friends
jokes.punchline: Homer's vague, insulting descriptions instead of actual names
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - crude but specific physical descriptions showing he notices but doesn't connect

jokes.text: Hank Jones.
jokes.setup: Pressed for an actual name of Bart's friend
jokes.punchline: Obviously made-up generic name
jokes.explanation: Homer's transparent lie with the most generic name possible - shows his panic and lack of knowledge

jokes.text: Steve McQueen. That's your hero.
jokes.setup: Asked who Bart's hero is
jokes.punchline: Homer projects his own hero onto his son, immediately corrected
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's self-centeredness and inability to see Bart as separate person - good character insight

jokes.text: He's always chewing on that phone cord. He hasn't done that since he was two.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to name Bart's hobbies
jokes.punchline: Reveals Homer's knowledge of Bart is eight years out of date
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Homer's cluelessness - his most recent memory of Bart is from toddlerhood

jokes.text: D'oh! That's a hobby.
jokes.setup: Discovers Bart building soap box derby car
jokes.punchline: Homer's delayed realization of obvious fact
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer beat - slow to process obvious information, uses signature catchphrase

jokes.text: You're also fat. / I'm also fat!
jokes.setup: Homer's self-deprecation
jokes.punchline: Selma adding insult, Homer accepting it as valid
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Homer's vulnerable state makes him accept any criticism

jokes.text: Oh, my God. I don't know jack about my boy. I'm a bad father. You're also fat. I'm also fat!
jokes.setup: Homer realizing he failed the fatherhood test
jokes.punchline: Patty piling on with weight insult, Homer accepting it immediately
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Homer's self-realization interrupted by cruel but accurate observation

jokes.text: Good Lord! A zero? Lana, cancel my 1:00.
jokes.setup: Homer got zero on fatherhood test
jokes.punchline: Institute treating it as unprecedented emergency requiring immediate attention
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist reaction treating Homer's failure as historic crisis - nice escalation

jokes.text: As three-time derby champ Ronnie Beck says... 'Poorly guarded construction sites are a gold mine.'
jokes.setup: Bart getting lumber for his car
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to theft disguised as wisdom from authority figure
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - theft advice presented as inspirational quote from sports hero

jokes.text: Bart, I think they're finally hauling your dad away. Maybe it's for the best.
jokes.setup: Station wagon arrives for Homer
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact acceptance that Homer being institutionalized might be good
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from Lisa treating Homer's removal as family improvement - perfectly deadpan

jokes.text: If he's as smart as he is funny, I'm sold.
jokes.setup: Given Bill Cosby's fatherhood book
jokes.punchline: Homer's simple logic equating comedy success with parenting wisdom
jokes.explanation: Homer's flawed but understandable logic about celebrity expertise - shows his simplistic thinking

jokes.text: That's easy for you to say... you preachy, egg-headed institute guy.
jokes.setup: Advice to spend time with his son
jokes.punchline: Homer's defensive hostility toward expert help he sought out
jokes.explanation: Homer's contradictory behavior - seeking help then resenting it, with specific insults

jokes.text: That back and forther you witnessed didn't happen overnight. It took years of effort.
jokes.setup: Father-son interaction between Dave and his son
jokes.punchline: Treating basic conversation as major achievement requiring years of work
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan humor treating mundane parent-child interaction as monumental accomplishment

jokes.text: What boy? Your son.
jokes.setup: Dave asking about boy's interests
jokes.punchline: Homer has already forgotten they're discussing his own son
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - losing track of conversation about his own child shows complete disconnection

jokes.text: Dear God! Not again!
jokes.setup: Homer mentions soap box derby
jokes.punchline: Dave's horrified reaction implies bad history with derby parents
jokes.explanation: Suggests Dave has dealt with soap box derby disasters before - nice character detail

jokes.text: I built a lot of things around the house-- the spice rack... the bird feeder... the Gymboree.
jokes.setup: Homer listing his construction achievements
jokes.punchline: Final item is clearly a major commercial franchise
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from small household items to major commercial enterprise - perfectly absurd

jokes.text: What was that? Ah, who cares?
jokes.setup: Something crashes or breaks
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate dismissal of obvious structural failure
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer response to his own incompetence - immediate denial and indifference

jokes.text: Cosby's first law of intergenerational perversity. No matter what you tell your child to do... he will always do the opposite.
jokes.setup: Homer reading parenting advice
jokes.punchline: Overly academic term for basic child psychology
jokes.explanation: Decent academic-sounding name for obvious parenting principle - moderately clever

jokes.text: That sounds too complicated. Okay, don't use reverse psychology. All right, I will.
jokes.setup: Homer dismissing reverse psychology as complicated
jokes.punchline: Immediately falls for reverse psychology about reverse psychology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke where reverse psychology is used to convince Homer to use reverse psychology - perfectly structured

jokes.text: Thank you, Bill Cosby. You've saved the Simpsons.
jokes.setup: Reverse psychology works on Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer crediting Cosby with saving his family/the show
jokes.explanation: Nice meta reference treating celebrity advice as salvation for both family and TV series

jokes.text: that Bart is a little miracle-- his winning smile, his button nose, his fat little stomach...
jokes.setup: Homer praising Bart poetically
jokes.punchline: List includes 'fat little stomach' as positive trait
jokes.explanation: Homer's crude but affectionate description mixing sweet and inappropriate observations

jokes.text: He reminds me of me before the weight of the world crushed my spirit.
jokes.setup: Homer reflecting on Bart
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly deep and depressing self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Homer showing rare genuine depth and melancholy self-reflection

jokes.text: He's my son, and if he doesn't wanna wear one, you can't make him.
jokes.setup: Official says Bart needs helmet
jokes.punchline: Homer defending Bart's right to be unsafe
jokes.explanation: Homer's misguided parental authority defending his son's right to danger - typical bad judgment

jokes.text: Ay, caramba!
jokes.setup: Seeing Martin's high-tech racer
jokes.punchline: Bart's signature exclamation of surprise
jokes.explanation: Bart's signature catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy

jokes.text: Whoa, talk about your pieces of crap.
jokes.setup: Nelson seeing the Simpson racer
jokes.punchline: Brutal honest assessment of their car
jokes.explanation: Nelson's characteristically blunt and cruel assessment - perfectly in voice

jokes.text: Careful, boys. It took me months to steal that bumper.
jokes.setup: Other kids mocking his racer
jokes.punchline: Martin casually admitting to theft with pride in the effort
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion - nerdy Martin revealing criminal dedication to his craft

jokes.text: It took me months to steal that bumper.
jokes.setup: Warning others to be careful around his car
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that bumper is stolen property
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson line - casual criminality presented as legitimate effort and skill

jokes.text: Help! Help! What was that? You didn't hear nothing!
jokes.setup: Someone calling for help in background
jokes.punchline: Nelson's immediate intimidation to cover ongoing crime
jokes.explanation: Good background gag showing Nelson's ongoing criminal activity during the race

jokes.text: As three-time soap box derby champion Ronnie Beck says... 'Gravity is my copilot.'
jokes.setup: Bart quoting his derby hero again
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'God is my copilot' with physics concept
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay callback combining religious expression with racing physics

jokes.text: Deploy, damn you! Deploy!
jokes.setup: Martin's high-tech car malfunctioning during crash
jokes.punchline: Desperate commands to unresponsive safety equipment
jokes.explanation: Good slapstick escalation with Martin's uncharacteristic profanity under stress

jokes.text: Save your palliative clichés for the next poor sap, Doctor.
jokes.setup: Doctor saying he's lucky to be alive
jokes.punchline: Martin's overly sophisticated dismissal of medical reassurance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin line - unnecessarily sophisticated vocabulary expressing bitter cynicism

jokes.text: That racer transformed me into the twisted creature... you see before you.
jokes.setup: Martin explaining his condition
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic description of minor injury effects
jokes.explanation: Martin's theatrical self-pity with melodramatic language - good character consistency

jokes.text: It's slow, it's ugly, it handles like a shopping cart.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining why he doesn't want to drive Homer's car
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment comparing car to shopping cart
jokes.explanation: Good specific comparison that's both funny and hurtful - shopping cart is perfect analogy

jokes.text: When did I teach you that? I picked it up somewhere.
jokes.setup: Bart says Homer taught him to win
jokes.punchline: Homer's confusion and Bart's vague admission he learned it elsewhere
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's cluelessness about his own influence and Bart's casual dishonesty

jokes.text: I'll just sit here in Li'l Lightnin' which the Simpsons built... and remember that for one brief shining moment... I had a son.
jokes.setup: Bart choosing to drive Martin's car instead
jokes.punchline: Homer's melodramatic martyrdom with Camelot reference
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with Homer's over-the-top self-pity and literary reference - very quotable

jokes.text: Maybe I'm being a little anal but barefoot season's coming up... and there's a world of rusty nails out there.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining her tetanus shot
jokes.punchline: Child using adult terminology for practical health planning
jokes.explanation: Lisa's precocious health consciousness with adult language - character appropriate

jokes.text: This is how you get your kicks, isn't it, Lisa?
jokes.setup: Homer taking Lisa to doctor
jokes.punchline: Homer treating routine medical care as deliberate torture
jokes.explanation: Homer's paranoid interpretation of normal parenting duties - shows his self-centeredness

jokes.text: You! Home wrecker!
jokes.setup: Homer seeing Martin at doctor's office
jokes.punchline: Dramatic accusation over soap box derby situation
jokes.explanation: Homer's melodramatic blame of Martin for family situation - good escalation

jokes.text: But I guess I was wrong. You are a bad father.
jokes.setup: Lisa defending Homer in the past
jokes.punchline: Lisa's final verdict that Homer is indeed a bad father
jokes.explanation: Lisa's devastating final judgment - carries weight because she's usually supportive

jokes.text: Shut up, Flanders. Flanders! / When should a boy start dating? / There are two schools of thought on the subject-- / Great! Thanks!
jokes.setup: Homer dismissing Flanders then needing him
jokes.punchline: Cutting off Flanders mid-explanation after getting what he needs
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer-Flanders dynamic - rudeness followed by exploitation

jokes.text: I'll curse that name till the day I die! Flanders! Flanders.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to remember who he talks to about parenting
jokes.punchline: Dramatic curse followed by realization Flanders is the answer
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from curse to realization - Homer's dramatic hatred turning to practical need

jokes.text: What did I just tell you? Kill spectators.
jokes.setup: Martin asking what he was just told
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual repetition of instruction to kill people
jokes.explanation: Dark humor callback with Bart casually stating deadly instruction as learned fact

jokes.text: Check out the rack on the blond in the fourth row.
jokes.setup: Mayor giving inspiring speech to racers
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual comment into live microphone
jokes.explanation: Classic politician hypocrisy - public propriety undermined by private vulgarity

jokes.text: The better man won, Simpson. You can really drive.
jokes.setup: Nelson apparently showing good sportsmanship
jokes.punchline: Seemingly genuine compliment from the bully
jokes.explanation: Unexpected character development showing Nelson capable of genuine respect

jokes.text: Ha-ha, ha-ha-ha. Loser! Na-na, na-na-na
jokes.setup: Nelson's moment of good sportsmanship
jokes.punchline: Immediate return to characteristic taunting
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - brief maturity immediately undermined by signature bullying behavior

jokes.text: You know, there is such a thing as being a bad winner.
jokes.setup: Bart and Homer celebrating obnoxiously
jokes.punchline: Marge's reasonable objection to poor sportsmanship
jokes.explanation: Standard Marge voice of reason moment - functional but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Mom, I never won before. I may never win again. Na-na, na-na-na
jokes.setup: Marge criticizing bad sportsmanship
jokes.punchline: Bart's reasonable justification followed by continued taunting
jokes.explanation: Good character logic - Bart makes valid point about rarity of winning then continues anyway

tmdb_overview: When Homer helps Bart build a soapbox racer, it does little for the car but a lot for their relationship.
insight: Racing subplot carries weak character work; mid-tier density masks uneven emotional tone.
